IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: A CENTER FOR EXHIBITIONS, INFORMATION, AND DOCUMENTATION ABOUT LIVING ARTISTS SINCE 1977, WHICH CONTINUES TO BE A PLACE OF EXPERIMENTATION AND A HUB OF NEW ART AND NEW IDEAS.
IRS990.Desc: EXHIBITIONS AND SPECIAL PROJECTS REFLECT THE NEW MUSEUM'S ENDURING COMMITMENT TO ENCOURAGING NEW WORK BY A DIVERSE RANGE OF EMERGING AND ESTABLISHED INTERNATIONAL ARTISTS. PROGRAMS CHANGE REGULARLY AND ENCAPSULATE THE NEW MUSEUM'S PHILOSOPHY OF OPENNESS AND FEARLESSNESS, AS WELL AS OUR HISTORY, AS THE HOME OF SOCIALLY COMMITTED CONTEMPORARY ART.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: NEW INC, THE FIRST MUSEUM-LED INCUBATOR, IS A SHARED WORKSPACE AND PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM DESIGNED TO SUPPORT CREATIVE PRACTITIONERS WORKING IN THE AREAS OF ART, TECHNOLOGY, AND DESIGN.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: THROUGH THE DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION AND PUBLIC ENGAGEMENTS RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT SEASONS, THE NEW MUSEUM'S PUBLIC PROGRAMMING CONNECTS VARIOUS PROJECTS IN THE MUSEUM, THEATER, 5TH FLOOR GALLERY, AND RESOURCE CENTER AROUND A NEW ORGANIZING THEME EACH FALL AND SPRING. ADDITIONALLY, A WIDE RANGE OF EXHIBITION-RELATED PROGRAMS INCLUDE FILM SCREENINGS, TALKS WITH ARTISTS AND SCHOLARS, CONFERENCES, PERFORMANCES, AND LIVE MUSIC THAT TAKES PLACE IN THE NEW MUSEUM THEATER.

Form990PartVIISectionAGrp.TitleTxt: CHIEF OF STAFF & VP OF STRATEGY

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule A, Part II, Line 10 Other Income
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: DESCRIPTION - OTHER, COLUMN A - 750183.0, COLUMN B - 91252.0, COLUMN C - 26848.0, COLUMN D - 631463.0, COLUMN E - 134416.0, COLUMN F - 1634162.0;

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule D, Part X, Line 2 ASC 740 FOOTNOTE
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: MANAGEMENT HAS EVALUATED THEIR INCOME TAX POSITIONS UNDER THE GUIDANCE INCLUDED IN ASC 740. BASED ON THEIR REVIEW, MANAGMENT HAS NOT IDENTIFIED ANY MATERIAL UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS TO BE RECORDED OR DISCLOSED IN THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule D, Part V, Line 4 Intended uses of endowment funds
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ENDOWMENT FUNDS ARE HELD IN INVESTMENT ACCOUNTS WITH AN APPROVED ANNUAL DRAW OF A PERCENTAGE OF EARNINGS TO SUPPORT MUSEUM ACTIVITIES AND PROGRAMS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule D, Part XI, Line 2(d) Other revenues in audited financial statements not in form 990
Schedule D, Part XII, Line 2(d) Other expenses in audited financial statements not in form 990

RltdOrgOfficerTrstKeyEmplGrp.TitleTxt: CHIEF OF STAFF & VP OF STRATEGY

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule M, Part I, Line 32b Third parties used to solicit, process, or sell noncash contributions
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FIRST REPUBLIC SECURITIES SELLS DONATED STOCK UPON RECEIPT.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule M, Part I Explanations of reporting method for number of contributions
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Securities - Publicly traded - THE ORGANIZATION IS REPORTING THE NUMBER OF COTNRIBUTIONS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part III, Line 1 ORGANIZATION'S MISSION
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE NEW MUSEUM IS THE ONLY MUSEUM DEVOTED EXCLUSIVELY TO CONTEMPORARY ART IN NEW YORK AND IS ONE OF THE LEADING CONTEMPORARY ART MUSEUMS WORLDWIDE. OUR MISSION IS SIMPLY NEW ART, NEW IDEAS WITH A COMMITMENT TO GLOBAL PROGRAMS. UNPARALLELED IN THE UNITED STATES, THE NEW MUSEUM OCCUPIES A UNIQUE AND IMPORTANT PLACE IN THE FIELD. WE ARE DISTINGUISHED BY A BOLD CULTURAL PROGRAM THAT PROVIDES OPPORTUNITIES FOR ARTISTS TO CREATE AND SHOW CONTEMPORARY ART IN A MUSEUM SETTING, WHILE ENGAGING THE ARTISTIC COMMUNITY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part III, Line 4d Description of other program services
Form 990, Part VI, Line 11b Review of form 990 by governing body
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE 990 IS REVIEWED AND APPROVED BY THE AUDIT COMMITTEE AND THEN SUBMITTED TO THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES PRIOR TO FILING. IF THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES HAVE ANY QUESTIONS THEY ARE ADDRESSED BY RICHARD DRYSDALE, VP OF FINANCE AND ADMINISTRATION.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 12c Conflict of interest policy
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL TRUSTEES AND EMPLOYEES ARE REQUIRED TO SIGN A CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY. THE PRESIDENT OF THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES AND THE DIRECTOR WILL REPORT TO THE BOARD AT LEAST ONCE ANNUALLY CONCERNING ANY DISCLOSURES OF POTENTIAL CONFLICTS OF INTEREST MADE TO THEM, AND ANY OTHER CONFLICTS OF INTEREST WHICH HAVE OCCURRED. IF ANY TRUSTEE OR EMPLOYEE HAS A CONFLICT, THEY WILL RECUSE THEMSELVES WHILE THE TOPIC IS DISCUSSED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 15a Process to establish compensation of top management official
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE DIRECTOR POSITION IS APPROVED BY THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES. THE BOARD MUST APPROVE THE SALARY OF THE DIRECTOR. THE 2024 SALARY INCLUDED AN INCREASE APPROVED BY THE BOARD IN 2023.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 15b Process to establish compensation of other employees
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE DIRECTOR MAY PROPOSE SALARIES FOR ALL OTHER KEY EMPLOYEES, BUT THE SALARY BUDGET MUST BE APPROVED BY THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES. THIS WAS DONE IN MAY 2023 FOR FY24.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 19 Required documents available to the public
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: DOCUMENTS ARE KEPT ON FILE AND ARE DISTRIBUTED UPON REQUEST. DOCUMENTS ARE ALSO AVAILABLE ON THE GUIDESTAR AND PRO PUBLICA WEBSITES FOR PUBLIC INFORMATION.
